summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV strikes on Russian infrastructure, including a railway in Zaporizhzhia and a fuel depot in Taganrog. Ukrainian SBS claimed destruction of a Russian Iskander missile system and two Tu‑142 aircraft at a Taganrog airfield. Russian‑linked sources warned of a possible large‑scale combined strike on Russian territory within 24‑48 hours.
key_points: Ukrainian SBS destroyed an Iskander missile complex and two Tu‑142 strategic bombers at a Taganrog airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67300).
Ukrainian drones hit a railway in Zaporizhzhia, killing a train driver and injuring two rail workers (Source: @UkraineNow/81554).
A Ukrainian drone strike damaged block 6 of the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, marking the first known targeted attack on core reactor equipment (Source: @tass_agency/378264).
Ukrainian forces attacked a fuel tanker, fuel reservoir and administration building in Taganrog, also striking an oil depot in Armavir and a depot in occupied Feodosia (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Multiple UAVs were reported over northern Ukrainian regions heading toward key towns such as Snovsk, Semenivka, Zhytomyr, and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/63325, @kpszsu/63358).
A Russian source warned of a mass combined strike on Russian territory planned within the next 24‑48 hours (Source: @eRadarrua/94367).
Greek artillery pieces (190) were transferred to Ukraine via the Czech Republic amid regional tensions (Source: @tass_agency/378258).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian SBS destroyed an Iskander missile system and two Tu‑142 aircraft at Taganrog, demonstrating deep‑strike capability. Ukrainian drones have repeatedly struck Russian infrastructure, including a railway in Zaporizhzhia and block 6 of the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant. Russian warnings of a large‑scale combined attack within 48 hours heighten tension on both sides.

points: Ukrainian SBS reported destroying an Iskander operational‑tactical missile complex and two Tu‑142 long‑range bombers at a military airfield in Taganrog, Rostov region, during a night operation on 30 May (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67300).

Ukrainian forces attacked a railway facility in Zaporizhzhia, killing the train driver and wounding two Ukrainian Railways employees; the strike also damaged an electric locomotive and a heat‑carrying train (Source: @UkraineNow/81554).

A UAV strike hit block 6 of the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, marking the first known targeted attack on core reactor equipment, according to Russian nuclear authority Likhachev (Source: @tass_agency/378264).

Ukrainian SBS claimed to have ignited a shadow‑fleet tanker and two oil depots in Taganrog and occupied Feodosia, as well as striking an oil depot in Armavir, Krasnodar Krai (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).

Multiple small UAVs were observed over northern Ukrainian oblasts, heading toward strategic towns such as Snovsk, Semenivka, Zhytomyr, and Sumy, indicating continued reconnaissance and harassment missions (Source: @kpszsu/63325, @kpszsu/63358).
Russian officials reported a planned mass combined strike on Russian territory within the next 24‑48 hours, according to intelligence sources (Source: @eRadarrua/94367).
Russia announced a ban on gasoline coupons and fuel canisters in Crimea effective 31 May, reflecting logistical restrictions in the occupied region (Source: @eRadarrua/94390).
Russian media cited a high threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from the Bryansk region, warning of potential attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67301).
Russia reported that the WhatsApp messenger faces fines totaling nearly 40 million rubles, indicating increased pressure on foreign communication tools (Source: @tass_agency/378263).

Greek artillery (190 pieces) was transferred to Ukraine via the Czech Republic, a move noted by Russian‑linked reporting as occurring amid Turkish threats to Greek sovereignty (Source: @tass_agency/378258).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Sumy region, including three drones approaching Konotop from the east and reconnaissance over Hlukhiv (Source: @eRadarrua/94374).
Ukrainian drones targeted infrastructure in Shostka, Sumy region, overnight (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30547).

Ukrainian SBS claimed to have damaged a fuel tanker and oil depot in Taganrog and a depot in occupied Feodosia, indicating deep strikes into Russian‑held eastern territories (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a railway in Zaporizhzhia, a strategic logistics hub in the southeast (Source: @UkraineNow/81554).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of possible large‑scale attacks on Russian territory, suggesting heightened activity on the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/94367).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over Chernihiv region heading toward Snovsk and Semenivka, indicating reconnaissance over the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/63325).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported over Kyiv region moving toward Zhytomyr, and over Sumy region moving toward Shostka (Source: @kpszsu/63358).
Ukrainian UAV groups from Bryansk region, Russia, were reported heading toward Sumy and Chernihiv, suggesting cross‑border incursions (Source: @kpszsu/63363).
Ukrainian SBS reported destroying Russian air assets at Taganrog, a strategic target on the northern Black Sea coast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67300).
Russian warnings of ballistic missile threats from the north, including Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk, affect northern defensive postures (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67301).
Ukrainian drones were reported near Kherson region heading toward Dnipropetrovsk, indicating pressure on the southern axis (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67312).

Ukrainian UAVs attacked a fuel tanker, fuel reservoir and administration building in Taganrog, as well as oil depots in Armavir and Feodosia, showing southern‑direction strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a railway in Zaporizhzhia, a key southern logistics hub (Source: @UkraineNow/81554).
Ukrainian SBS claimed to have ignited a shadow‑fleet tanker and oil depots in the southern occupied territories, demonstrating reach into the Black Sea coast (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of possible large‑scale combined attacks, potentially affecting southern command structures (Source: @eRadarrua/94367).
Ukrainian SBS reported destroying an Iskander missile system and two Tu‑142 strategic bombers at a Taganrog airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67300).

Ukrainian UAVs conducted multiple strikes on Russian infrastructure, including a railway in Zaporizhzhia and block 6 of the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant (Source: @UkraineNow/81554, @tass_agency/378264).
Russian sources warned of imminent mass combined strikes, implying potential use of air assets within 24‑48 hours (Source: @eRadarrua/94367).
Reports of high threat levels for Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk indicate active Russian air‑defence concerns (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67301).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted over multiple northern oblasts, suggesting ongoing low‑altitude air operations (Source: @kpszsu/63325, @kpszsu/63358).

key_developments: Ukrainian SBS destroyed an Iskander missile complex and two Tu‑142 aircraft at Taganrog airfield, demonstrating deep‑strike UAV capability. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67300)
Ukrainian drones struck a railway in Zaporizhzhia, killing a train driver and injuring two rail workers, and damaged rolling stock. (Source: @UkraineNow/81554)
A UAV attack hit block 6 of the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, the first known targeted strike on core reactor equipment. (Source: @tass_agency/378264)
Ukrainian forces ignited a shadow‑fleet tanker, fuel reservoir, and administration building in Taganrog, and struck oil depots in Armavir and occupied Feodosia. (Source: @UkraineNow/81546)
Russian intelligence warned of a mass combined strike on Russian territory planned within the next 24‑48 hours. (Source: @eRadarrua/94367)
Multiple UAVs were observed over northern Ukrainian regions heading toward strategic towns such as Snovsk, Semenivka, Zhytomyr, and Sumy. (Source: @kpszsu/63325, @kpszsu/63358)
Greek artillery (190 pieces) was transferred to Ukraine via the Czech Republic amid regional security tensions. (Source: @tass_agency/378258)
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations have expanded to deep strikes against Russian air assets and critical infrastructure, increasing pressure on Russian logistics and energy facilities. Russian warnings of a large‑scale combined attack suggest preparation for a high‑intensity response, potentially involving missile and air assets. The proliferation of UAV activity across northern and southern fronts indicates a broadening of the aerial contest, raising the risk of escalation.
sections.regional_response: NATO allies have highlighted the need for increased defensive support, as evidenced by discussions of drone deliveries and artillery transfers, while Russian authorities impose logistical restrictions in occupied Crimea and warn of ballistic missile threats. Neighboring states, such as Greece, are adjusting arms exports to Ukraine, reflecting shifting regional security calculations. Diplomatic rhetoric from Russian officials underscores a willingness to leverage hybrid tactics, including potential large‑scale strikes.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring shows a surge in UAV sightings over both frontlines, suggesting coordinated reconnaissance and strike missions. The reported destruction of high‑value Russian air assets indicates effective Ukrainian targeting intelligence. Russian statements about imminent mass attacks may be aimed at deterrence or psychological operations, requiring close watch for any escalation in missile or aerial activity.
